input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

data.content: BenchmarkDotNet doesn't recognize `net10.0` yet. I'll run each TFM separately.
data.reasoningText:
BenchmarkDotNet doesn't support net10.0 as a runtime moniker yet. I'll need to use a different approach - run the benchmarks separately for each TFM using the `--framework` flag and the default runtime. Let me use `--runtimes net90` for the .NET 9 run and just run on the host for .NET 10.

Actually, let me think about this differently. Since BenchmarkDotNet 0.14.x doesn't know about net10.0, I can:
1. Run the benchmark host on net10.0 but use `--runtimes net90` - this might work if BDN can resolve net9.0 runtime.


2. Or, build and run separately for each TFM without cross-runtime comparison.

I think option 2 is cleaner - I'll just run each TFM independently.

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-dbba97fa3bd44b99b731f85d3108f687/Benchmarks && dotnet run -c Release --framework net9.0 -- --filter '*' 2>&1", "description": "Run benchmarks on .NET 9", "initial_wait": 120, "mode": "sync"}
